rights.title: Voter Rights
rights.subtitle: Understand your constitutional rights and protections as an Indian voter.

booth.title: Find Your Booth
booth.subtitle: Enter your pincode or use GPS to locate your assigned polling station.
booth.boothData: Booth data is sourced from the Election Commission of India.

onboarding.hookTitle:
Your Vote.
Your Voice.
Your Right.
onboarding.locationSub: We need your location to show the right candidates for your constituency.
onboarding.gpsError: Could not access location. Enter your pincode.

facts.fact1: You can still vote if your name is on the electoral roll but you don't have a Voter ID card. You can use alternative documents like an Aadhaar card or PAN card.
facts.fact2: NOTA (None of the Above) option was introduced in Indian elections after the Supreme Court ruling in 2013. It allows voters to reject all candidates.
facts.fact3: A candidate's criminal record, assets, and educational qualifications are public information, mandated by the Supreme Court of India.
facts.fact4: No voter can be turned away from a polling booth after the queue closes. If you are in line before the official closing time, you have the right to vote.
facts.fact5: The Model Code of Conduct restricts political parties from using government machinery for campaigning once elections are announced.
